source.body: ONS Oncology Nursing Society Infection Prevention and Control Clinical Practice Guidelines
source.current_value: Evidence insufficient for categorical restriction of raw produce in all neutropenic patients; fresh flower/plant restriction in PE rooms retains CDC support; raw-produce restriction is institution-specific policy, not universal evidence-based standard.

source.body: CDC 2019 Guidelines for Isolation Precautions: Preventing Transmission of Infectious Agents in Healthcare Settings — Appendix A Protective Environment
source.current_value: Plants and flowers should be avoided in Protective Environment rooms (Aspergillus spore risk); raw-produce restriction not universally mandated by CDC at the Isolation Precautions level.

source.body: CDC 2019 Guidelines for Isolation Precautions: Preventing Transmission of Infectious Agents in Healthcare Settings — Appendix A, Table 4: Sequence for Putting On and Removing PPE
source.current_value: CDC 2019 recommended doffing sequence: Remove gloves → Remove gown → Perform hand hygiene → Remove face shield or goggles → Remove mask or respirator → Perform hand hygiene. Item teaches: Gloves → Goggles → Gown → Mask → Hand hygiene (last only). Gown should precede goggles; intermediate hand hygiene after gown removal is absent from the item.
